keyMyths.title: The Great Renunciation
keyMyths.description: Siddhartha Gautama, a prince sheltered from suffering, witnesses the realities of old age, sickness, and death, leading him to abandon his privileged life in search of enlightenment.

keyMyths.title: Defeat of Mara
keyMyths.description: As Siddhartha meditates under the Bodhi tree, Mara, the demon of illusion and death, attacks him with temptations and armies, but Siddhartha remains steadfast, overcoming Mara's forces through his unwavering resolve.

keyMyths.title: The First Sermon
keyMyths.description: After achieving enlightenment, the Buddha travels to Sarnath and delivers his first sermon, setting in motion the Wheel of Dharma and introducing the Four Noble Truths and the Eightfold Path to his first disciples.

keyMyths.title: Taming Nalagiri
keyMyths.description: Devadatta, the Buddha's jealous cousin, unleashes a drunken elephant named Nalagiri upon the Buddha, but the Buddha calms the raging beast with his compassion and power, demonstrating his mastery over anger and violence.

keyMyths.title: The Parinirvana
keyMyths.description: The Buddha, knowing his time is near, travels to Kushinagar and peacefully enters Parinirvana (final nirvana), teaching his disciples about impermanence and the importance of self-reliance until his last breath.

achievements: Achieved enlightenment under the Bodhi tree, becoming the Buddha.
Formulated the Four Noble Truths, providing a framework for understanding suffering and its cessation.
Established the Eightfold Path, a practical guide for achieving liberation.
Founded the Sangha, the monastic community that preserves and propagates the Buddha's teachings.
Traveled extensively throughout India, teaching and inspiring countless individuals.
Converted kings and commoners alike to the path of Dharma.
Demonstrated compassion and wisdom in all his actions and teachings.

sources.name: Tripitaka (Pali Canon)
sources.description: The earliest collection of Buddhist texts, containing the Buddha's teachings and monastic rules.
sources.title: Tripitaka (Pali Canon)

sources.name: Buddhacharita
sources.description: An epic poem by Ashvaghosha recounting the life of the Buddha.
sources.title: Buddhacharita

sources.name: Lalitavistara Sutra
sources.description: A Mahayana Buddhist sutra that elaborates on the Buddha's life and enlightenment.
sources.title: Lalitavistara Sutra

sources.name: Jataka Tales
sources.description: A collection of stories about the Buddha's previous lives, illustrating the development of his virtues.
sources.title: Jataka Tales

sources.name: Mahavamsa
sources.description: A historical chronicle of Sri Lanka, containing accounts of the spread of Buddhism.
sources.title: Mahavamsa

sources.name: Vinaya Pitaka
sources.description: The section of the Tripitaka dealing with monastic rules and discipline.
sources.title: Vinaya Pitaka
